search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk ECG result review clinician interpretation AF chest pain
candidate_sources_rejected: generic result-review text implying normality or diagnosis
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: ECG interpretation requires the actual tracing, technical quality, clinical context, and qualified clinician review.
No rhythm, conduction, ischemia, normality, urgency, or treatment conclusion was generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults with an ECG being reviewed; individual source applicability depends on suspected AF, chest pain, or bradycardia context.
setting_applicability: Primary, outpatient, urgent, or specialist result review; the workflow does not replace formal ECG interpretation or acute care.
UAE_applicability: International guidance requires UAE ECG reporting, credentialing, emergency, referral, and facility-policy adaptation.
recency_verification: NICE NG196, NICE CG95, and ACC/AHA/HRS 2018 sources were reviewed on 2026-07-13; CG95 and ACC recency gaps remain.
superseded_check: The reviewed sources remain current for their conditional scopes; no single universal ECG-result guideline was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The NICE AF section supports documenting the ECG source and date, reason for review, related symptoms, pulse or rhythm context, and clinician interpretation where AF is suspected; it does not infer an ECG diagnosis.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician assessment of relevant symptoms, pulse, ECG, and ambulatory ECG context without automatically diagnosing atrial fibrillation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The NICE chest-pain information section supports documenting clinician explanation of a reviewed test, uncertainty, questions, shared decisions, safety-netting, and follow-up without generating an interpretation or advice.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting concerns, uncertainty, questions, explanation of reviewed tests, shared decisions, and clinician-provided advice for recurrent pain without generating patient instructions.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The bradycardia algorithm was reviewed to confirm ECG relevance for suspected bradycardia but was not generalized to every ECG result.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports comprehensive clinician history and physical examination, ECG, directed blood testing, symptom frequency, selected ambulatory monitoring, and reversible or treatable cause context without generating a diagnosis or test order.

unresolved_source_gaps: Indication, date, technical quality, calibration, rate, rhythm, axis, intervals, waves, segments, comparison tracing, symptoms, formal interpretation, diagnosis, urgency, treatment, referral, communication, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
